question: Create a `Coordinate` class with `x` and `y` integer members. Implement `getX()` and `getY()` as `const` member functions. Demonstrate how a `const` object of `Coordinate` can only call its `const` member functions.

test_cases.expected:
Mutable Coordinate (initial): (10, 20)
Mutable Coordinate (after setX): (15, 20)
Fixed Coordinate (const object): (30, 40)
Fixed Coordinate X (using getX()): 30
Fixed Coordinate Y (using getY()): 40
Attempting to call setX() on 'fixedCoord' (a const object) would result in a compile-time error, as expected.
